user_query:
Please read the files under input/ (brief.md, guest_list.csv, preferences.yaml, venue_shortlist.json, program_outline.md) and plan a 200‑attendee corporate product launch event for late September.

Create the following outputs under output/:
1) output/plan.md — A comprehensive event plan using these exact section headers: "Date & Time", "Venue (Primary & Backup)", "Expected Attendance", "Budget Summary", "Theme", "Timeline". The Timeline section must include markers labeled exactly: "8 weeks:", "6 weeks:", "4 weeks:", "2 weeks:", "1 week:", and "Day of:" with relevant actions. For Venue, select one primary and one backup option from input/venue_shortlist.json, referencing their names and addresses. Ensure the proposed date is a late-September weekend.
2) output/budget.csv — A budget breakdown with headers: category, percent, amount_usd. Use the total budget cap from input/preferences.yaml. Allocate categories and percentages so the sum is 100%, and keep these categories within recommended ranges: Venue 30–40%, Catering 25–35%, Photography 10–15%, Floral/Decor 5–10%, Misc 10–15%. Include at least these five categories. Compute amount_usd = percent * total_budget / 100, rounded to whole dollars.
3) output/checklist.md — A preparation checklist with three sections titled exactly: "Pre-Event", "Day-Of", and "Post-Event". Each section must list at least 4 actionable items (bulleted) tailored to the program in input/program_outline.md and any must‑have requirements in input/preferences.yaml.
4) output/invitation_text.md — Draft invitation copy that includes the event name from input/brief.md, the proposed late-September date and time, the selected venue address (matching one from input/venue_shortlist.json), RSVP instructions including the RSVP email from input/brief.md, and a single-sentence dress code drawn from input/preferences.yaml.
5) output/vendor_list.csv — A vendor shortlist with headers: name, category, contact, notes. Provide at least 10 vendors spanning relevant categories (Catering, Photography, Floral, AV, Venue, Other). Each row must have a contact (email or phone) and a short note referencing suitability for this event.
6) output/tasks.json — A structured task tracking file with keys: tasks (array of objects) and stats (object). Each task object must include: title, due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), priority (low|medium|high), status (todo|in_progress|done), owner, tags (array). Include at least 15 tasks, at least 3 high priority. The stats object must include: total, by_status (counts for each status), and by_priority (counts for each priority). Ensure stats match the tasks array.

Use guest_list.csv and brief.md to inform expected attendance (report a numeric estimate in plan.md) and team owners for tasks. Justify venue selection in plan.md based on capacity, availability, and cost range from venue_shortlist.json.

Write all outputs only under output/ with the exact filenames specified.
